hidden_state.scenario_summary: Invoice confusion is the clearest customer-facing problem, while a brief operational note adds noise without resolving whether a commercial adjustment is due.
latent_facts: The customer is primarily asking about an unexpected invoice preview.
A short degradation note exists, but it is too weak to support an SLA remedy.
The case should route to finance review without overcommitting to a root cause.
likely_failure_modes: The model over-indexes on the incident note and treats this as an outage review.
The model assumes an invoice adjustment is definitely due instead of preserving uncertainty.

case_packet.title: Invoice preview mismatch with weak operational noise

notes: Customer is focused on an invoice preview that looks materially higher than expected.
No prior pricing-config mismatch on record.

pricing_notes: Invoice preview shows burst usage above commitment.
Dashboard explanation for the burst line items is incomplete.

usage_record.telemetry_summary: A short degraded-performance notice overlapped with the billing complaint, but usage stayed largely stable.
anomalies: Customer-facing invoice explanation did not reconcile to burst line items.

incident_record.customer_impact_summary: Brief slowdown reported, but customer mainly complained about billing.
incident_record.engineering_summary: Minor capacity pressure was observed but resolved quickly and did not cross breach thresholds.

case_packet.customer_note: The invoice preview is much higher than we expected, and we also noticed a brief slowdown earlier that day. Please explain whether either of these changed what we owe.
case_packet.policy_snippet: Commercial review should confirm whether pricing explanation gaps reflect a true discrepancy before applying invoice adjustments. Short degradations below SLA thresholds do not automatically trigger credits.
case_packet.calculator_output: Burst usage detected, but current calculator output does not determine whether the preview discrepancy reflects billable overage or explanation gap.
case_packet.visible_case_summary: Billing confusion is primary, but a short incident note introduces irrelevant operational noise.

adjudication_notes: The billing signal is stronger than the operational signal, but the packet does not justify a final invoice adjustment decision.
The short degradation should not dominate routing.
ground_truth.reference_customer_note: We found an unresolved mismatch between the invoice preview and the explanation available in the current billing records, so we are routing this for finance review before giving you a final answer. At this stage, we are not treating it as an SLA credit case.
ground_truth.reference_internal_note: Owner: shared_revops_finance. Action: hold_for_finance_review because the strongest unresolved signal is invoice_confusion, root cause is still unknown, and the brief operational note does not support a breach finding.
